name: intentional-core
documentation:
# Intentional - Core package

[![PyPI - Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/intentional-core.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/intentional-core)
[![PyPI - Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/intentional-core.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/intentional-core)

-----

## Table of Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [License](#license)

## Installation

```console
pip install intentional-core
```

## License

`intentional` is distributed under the terms of the [AGPL](LICENSE.txt) license.

SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-or-later

documentation: Handle the event.
Listens to events and handles them.

documentation: Register the listener.

methods.name: intentional_core.events.EventEmitter.emit
documentation: Send the event to the listener.

Sends any event to the listener.
TODO see if there's any scenario where we need more as this pattern is easy to extend but can get messy.

modules.name: intentional_core.model_client
documentation: Functions to load model client classes from config files.
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2024-present ZanSara <github@zansara.dev>
SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-or-later

functions.name: intentional_core.model_client.load_model_client_from_dict
documentation:
Load a model client from a dictionary configuration.

Args:
    config: The configuration dictionary.

Returns:
    The ModelClient instance.

variables.name: intentional_core.model_client._MODELCLIENT_CLASSES
documentation: This is a global dictionary that maps model client names to their classes

classes.name: intentional_core.model_client.ModelClient

methods.name: intentional_core.model_client.ModelClient.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the model client.

Args:
    parent: The parent bot structure.

methods.name: intentional_core.model_client.ModelClient.connect
documentation: Connect to the model.

methods.name: intentional_core.model_client.ModelClient.disconnect
documentation: Disconnect from the model.

methods.name: intentional_core.model_client.ModelClient.run
documentation:
Handle events from the model by either processing them internally or by translating them into higher-level
events that the BotStructure class can understand, then re-emitting them.

methods.name: intentional_core.model_client.ModelClient.send
documentation: Send a unit of data to the model. The response is streamed out as an async generator.

methods.name: intentional_core.model_client.ModelClient.handle_interruption
documentation:
Handle an interruption while rendering the output to the user.

Args:
    lenght_to_interruption: The length of the data that was produced to the user before the interruption.
        This value could be number of characters, number of words, milliseconds, number of audio frames, etc.
        depending on the bot structure that implements it.

class_variables.name: intentional_core.model_client.ModelClient.name
documentation:
The name of the client. This should be a unique identifier for the client type.
This string will be used in configuration files to identify the type of client to serve a model from.

Tiny base class used to recognize Intentional model clients.

In order for your client to be usable, you need to assign a value to the `name` class variable
in the client class' definition.

classes.name: intentional_core.model_client.TurnBasedModelClient
documentation: Base class for model clients that support turn-based message exchanges, as opposed to continuous streaming of data.

classes.name: intentional_core.model_client.ContinuousStreamModelClient
documentation: Base class for model clients that support continuous streaming of data, as opposed to turn-based message exchanges.

modules.name: intentional_core.end_conversation
documentation:
Tool that ends and resets the conversation.
Once a conversation reaches this stage, the bot should restart from the _start_ stage. See IntentRouter.
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2024-present ZanSara <github@zansara.dev>
SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-or-later

classes.name: intentional_core.end_conversation.EndConversationTool

methods.name: intentional_core.end_conversation.EndConversationTool.run
documentation: Ends the conversation and resets the intent router to its initial stage.
Tool to end the conversation. Resets the intent router to its initial stage.

functions.name: intentional_core.bot_interface.load_configuration_file

Load an Intentional bot from a YAML configuration file.

Args:
    path: Path to the YAML configuration file.

Returns:
    The bot instance.

functions.name: intentional_core.bot_interface.load_bot_interface_from_dict
documentation:
Load a bot interface, and all its inner classes, from a dictionary configuration.

Args:
    config: The configuration dictionary.

Returns:
    The bot interface instance.

variables.name: intentional_core.bot_interface._BOT_INTERFACES
documentation: This is a global dictionary that maps bot interface names to their classes

classes.name: intentional_core.bot_interface.BotInterface

methods.name: intentional_core.bot_interface.BotInterface.run
documentation:
Run the bot interface.

This method should be overridden by the subclass to implement the bot's main loop.

class_variables.name: intentional_core.bot_interface.BotInterface.name
documentation:
The name of the bot interface. This should be a unique identifier for the bot interface.
This string will be used in configuration files to identify the bot interface.

The bot interface name should directly recall the class name as much as possible.
For example, the name of "LocalBotInterface" should be "local", the name of "WhatsappBotInterface" should be
"whatsapp", etc.

Tiny base class used to recognize Intentional bots interfaces.

The class name is meant to represent the **communication channel** you will use to interact with your bot.
For example an interface that uses a local command line interface would be called "TerminalBotInterface", one that
uses Whatsapp would be called "WhatsappBotInterface", one that uses Twilio would be called "TwilioBotInterface",
etc.

In order for your bot to be usable, you need to assign a value to the `name` class variable in the class definition.

Load a bot structure from a dictionary configuration.

variables.name: intentional_core.bot_structure._BOT_STRUCTURES
documentation: This is a global dictionary that maps bot structure names to their classes

classes.name: intentional_core.bot_structure.BotStructure

methods.name: intentional_core.bot_structure.BotStructure.__init__
documentation: Initialize the bot structure.

methods.name: intentional_core.bot_structure.BotStructure.connect
documentation: Connect to the bot.

methods.name: intentional_core.bot_structure.BotStructure.disconnect
documentation: Disconnect from the bot.

methods.name: intentional_core.bot_structure.BotStructure.run
documentation: Main loop for the bot.

methods.name: intentional_core.bot_structure.BotStructure.send
documentation: Send a message to the bot.

methods.name: intentional_core.bot_structure.BotStructure.handle_interruption
documentation:
Handle an interruption in the streaming.

Args:
    lenght_to_interruption: The length of the data that was produced to the user before the interruption.
        This value could be number of characters, number of words, milliseconds, number of audio frames, etc.
        depending on the bot structure that implements it.

methods.name: intentional_core.bot_structure.BotStructure.add_event_handler
documentation:
Add an event handler for a specific event type.

Args:
    event_name: The name of the event to handle.
    handler: The handler function to call when the event is received.

methods.name: intentional_core.bot_structure.BotStructure.handle_event
documentation: Handle different types of events that the model may generate.

class_variables.name: intentional_core.bot_structure.BotStructure.name
documentation:
The name of this bot's structure. This should be a unique identifier for the bot structure type.

The bot structure's name should directly recall the class name as much as possible. For example, the name of
"WebsocketBotStructure" should be "websocket", the name of "AudioToTextBotStructure" should be "audio_to_text",
etc.

Tiny base class used to recognize Intentional bot structure classes.

The bot structure's name is meant to represent the **structure** of the bot. For example a bot that uses a direct
WebSocket connection to a model such as OpenAI's Realtime API could be called "RealtimeAPIBotStructure", one that
uses a VAD-STT-LLM-TTS stack could be called "AudioToTextBotStructure", and so on

In order for your bot structure to be usable, you need to assign a value to the `name` class variable in the bot
structure class' definition.

classes.name: intentional_core.bot_structure.ContinuousStreamBotStructure
documentation: Base class for structures that support continuous streaming of data, as opposed to turn-based message exchanges.

classes.name: intentional_core.bot_structure.TurnBasedBotStructure
documentation: Base class for structures that support turn-based message exchanges, as opposed to continuous streaming of data.

methods.name: intentional_core.intent_routing.IntentRouter.run
documentation:
Given the response's classification, returns the new system prompt and the tools accessible in this stage.
